title: Why write tests when changing code?
prompt_md: You need to refactor a payments module that other teams depend on. Why is having a good automated test suite especially valuable here?
options: Tests make the production code run faster
Tests remove the need for code review entirely
Tests guarantee the code has zero bugs forever
Tests catch regressions, document intended behavior, and let you refactor safely with fast feedback
explanation_md: Tests give you the confidence to change code: they catch **regressions** the moment behavior drifts, they act as executable **documentation** of intended behavior, and they enable **safe refactoring** with fast feedback. They do not prove the absence of all bugs, speed up runtime, or replace code review.
